objective:
FICHE_STRATEGIE v1
id: breakout_donchian_adx
archetype: breakout_donchian_adx
family: breakout
timeframe: 1h
side: both
indicators:
- donchian(period=25)
- adx(period=14)
- atr(period=14)
entry:
- long: close > donchian.upper and adx.adx > 30
- short: close < donchian.lower and adx.adx > 30
exit:
- condition: cross_any(close, donchian.middle) or adx.adx < 25
risk:
stop_atr_mult: 1.75
tp_atr_mult: 5.5
description: ATR stop-loss (1.75x) and take-profit (5.5x)
constraints:
- no_lookahead: true
- only_registry_indicators: true

iterations.hypothesis: ATR-based stop-loss and take-profit strategies with Donchian bands and ADX indicators can improve trading results by reducing false signals in trending markets.

code.validation_error: [SIG001] `for i in range(...)` interdit dans la logique Builder.
code.validation_error_retry: [SIG001] `for i in range(...)` interdit dans la logique Builder.

iterations.hypothesis: This strategy will generate more profitable trades by using a combination of RSI, Bollinger Bands and ATR. This approach minimizes false signals while capturing trendier price action.

Using Donchian bands with an ADX indicator to identify trending markets and ATR based risk management for a potential trade entry. The breakout of the upper band when combined with a strong trend indicated by the ADX serves as our long signal, while the opposite occurs for short positions.

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] This iteration is focusing on using Donchian bands with an ADX indicator to identify trending markets. The strategy uses ATR based risk management for potential trade entry and exit. However, the backtest has not been completed yet. Possible weaknesses include overfitting of the model due to use of a large number of indicators, lack of consideration for other factors like liquidity or order book data which could affect market movement etc. If results are poor (negative return), reducing the complexity of the strategy by removing unnecessary indicators might be considered as well as testing different parameters for the ATR based risk management system.
[Backup plan] If results are poor (negative return) during this iteration, we can consider following actions: 1. Reduce the number of indicators used in the model to check if it has any impact on performance. This could help eliminate overfitting and improve accuracy; 2. Test different parameter values for ATR based risk management system - such as adjusting stop-loss or take-profit levels, changing lookahead times etc., which may lead to better results.
